name: linDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream
title: linDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream
syntax: dword linDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte dataBuffer[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp, dword timeoutPrevention);
description: Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a variable bit stream.

parameters.name: byteIndex
parameters.description: Start disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte

parameters.name: bitIndex
parameters.description: The index of the bit where the interrupting bit stream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255

parameters.name: bitStream
parameters.description: The bit stream to be used for the interruption. Maximum number of bits: 2^31-1.

parameters.name: lengthInNS
parameters.description: The length of each bit in nanoseconds.

parameters.name: numberOfBits
parameters.description: The number of bits in the bitStream-array. Note that while the dataBuffer-array will usually have a size of ceil (numberOfBits / 8), the size of lengthInNS will need to be at least numberOfBits.

parameters.name: roundup
parameters.description: If true, the lengths specified in lengthInNS will be rounded up to the next possible length that can be transmitted by the LIN hardware, otherwise the lengths will be rounded down.

parameters.name: timeoutPrevention
parameters.description: 0: deactivates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver. 1: activates the timeout prevention for the 7259- or 7269-transceiver.

return: On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.
availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
